front: What are inclusion bodies and how are they handled?
back: Inclusion bodies (IBs) are dense, insoluble aggregates of misfolded recombinant protein in E. coli. They form when expression rate exceeds folding capacity. Recovery: 1) Cell lysis, 2) IB washing (remove membrane proteins), 3) Solubilization (8M urea/GdnHCl), 4) Refolding (dialysis/dilution), 5) Purification.
